title: tick-cap hit logs no room/turn context — desync silent to ops
evidence: if (ticks >= MAX_TICKS) { console.error('NetworkClient.tickToCompletion: hit tick cap — possible corrupt engine state'); } — no roomId, no seq, no turn number, no action type that triggered it
impact: A tick-cap hit is the primary observable signal of engine desync. Without room/seq/action context the log entry cannot be correlated to a specific room or action, making it impossible to reproduce from logs alone.
recommendation: Add roomId, the action that triggered the replay (type+seq), and the current turn to the error log: console.error('tickToCompletion: hit tick cap', { roomId: this.roomId, turn: this.engine.getState().turn, seq: this.nextExpectedSeq - 1 })
acceptance_criteria: Log includes the seq of the action being replayed

title: rate-limit fail-open emits no structured signal — limiter bypass invisible
evidence: if (error) { console.error('rate_limit rpc error:', error.message); return true // fail open } — bare string log, no bucket name, no IP, no function context
impact: When the rate-limit RPC errors (DB hiccup, missing table), every subsequent request is silently allowed. The only record is a bare error.message with no bucket, IP, or function name — impossible to distinguish a limiter failure from any other DB error in Supabase's combined log stream.
recommendation: Log structured context: console.error('rate_limit: fail-open', { bucket, ip, error: error.message }). Consider also returning a counter or flag in the 200 response body so callers can detect unusual volume.
acceptance_criteria: Log is distinguishable from other DB errors without string matching

title: submit_action 409 seq-conflict not distinguishable in server logs from other errors
evidence: if (error.code === '23505') { return json({ ok: false, error: 'seq_conflict', retry: true }, 409) } — the 409 path has NO console.log/console.error; only the generic 500 path logs. A flood of seq-conflicts (e.g., a misbehaving client hammering fire) produces zero server-side signal.
impact: Seq-conflict rate is the clearest indicator of a client race or a misbehaving actor abusing the retry path. Without any log, elevated conflict rates are invisible until they show up as user-reported stuck turns.
recommendation: Add a console.log at INFO level on the 409 path: console.log('submit_action: seq_conflict', { roomId, playerId: (playerId as string)?.slice(0,8), seq: room?.turn }). Keep it below error severity since conflicts are expected and self-healing.
acceptance_criteria: 409 path emits at least one log line per conflict

title: no global unhandled-rejection / uncaught-error handler in main.ts
evidence: bootstrap() is called at line 521 with no window.addEventListener('unhandledrejection') or window.addEventListener('error') anywhere in the file. createClient() at line 442 is awaited inside startGame() but startGame() is called via void startGame(config) (line 394) — any exception thrown inside crashes silently.
impact: Any unhandled promise rejection (e.g., nc.initialize() throwing, a failed Supabase fetch) produces a blank-screen freeze with no user-visible message and no log record beyond the browser console — invisible on mobile. A desync crash leaves the player with a frozen game and no indication of what happened.
recommendation: Add window.addEventListener('unhandledrejection', e => { console.error('unhandledrejection:', e.reason); hud?.flashMessage('Unexpected error — please reload.'); }) after the bootstrap() call. Optionally wire to a lightweight error beacon.
acceptance_criteria: Unhandled rejections surface a user-visible message
Unhandled rejections emit a console.error with the reason

title: resyncLog failure is swallowed — missed-action recovery silently no-ops
evidence: if (error) { console.error('NetworkClient.resyncLog: failed to re-fetch log:', error.message); return; } — returns without setting any connection state or notifying the UI. resyncLog is called both on reconnection (line 268) AND inside the fire-watchdog recovery (line 559).
impact: If resyncLog fails (network down, Supabase outage), the recovery path silently returns. On the fire-watchdog path this means _isFiring stays true and the player is permanently stuck in 'Sending…' with no retry prompt and no UI signal beyond the connection banner if the Realtime socket also dropped.
recommendation: On resyncLog failure, call failFire('Network error — shot may not have sent. Try again.') if _isFiring is still set, and ensure setConnection('reconnecting') is also emitted so the UI shows the disconnect banner even when only the fetch leg fails.
acceptance_criteria: A resyncLog failure while _isFiring is true releases the fire lock and notifies the player
The log line includes roomId for correlation

title: submit_action rejection of 'Not your turn' silently dropped — wrong-turn client never logged
evidence: } else if (!isConflict && data.error !== 'Not your turn') { console.error('NetworkClient: submit_action rejected:', data.error) } — the 'Not your turn' branch is explicitly excluded from logging. No log, no UI signal.
impact: A client that submits on another player's turn (client-side turn-guard bug or desync) will silently receive a 403 and drop it. There is no diagnostic signal that the client's local engine disagrees with the server's referee cursor — the very condition that indicates a desync.
recommendation: Log 'Not your turn' rejections at warn level with roomId and local turn state: console.warn('submit_action: not-your-turn rejection — possible desync', { roomId: this.roomId, localActive: this.engine.getState().activePlayerId }). Do not surface to the user.
acceptance_criteria: A 'Not your turn' rejection emits at least a console.warn
Log includes roomId and local activePlayerId for desync diagnosis

title: edge functions log bare errors without room/player correlation context
evidence: Pattern across all functions: console.error('submit_action: fetch error', fetchError) — the roomId and playerId are in scope at that point but not included in the log call. Example from submit_action line 115-116: if (fetchError) { console.error('submit_action: fetch error', fetchError) } — roomId available from outer scope.
impact: Supabase Edge Function logs aggregate all invocations. A DB error log without roomId cannot be correlated to a specific stuck game. For a hobby project this is low severity, but distinguishing 'one room broke' from 'all rooms broke' requires roomId in the log.
recommendation: Thread roomId (and optionally anonymized playerId) into each console.error call after the variables are validated: console.error('submit_action: fetch error', { roomId, error: fetchError.message }). One-liner change per function.
acceptance_criteria: All console.error calls in edge functions include roomId when available

title: pendingActions buffer growth on gap — no diagnostic when seq gap stalls indefinitely
evidence: this.pendingActions.set(row.seq, row.action as NetworkAction); this.flushPendingActions(); — there is no check or log if pendingActions grows beyond a threshold or if a gap (missing seq) persists beyond a timeout. The turn-stall watchdog (TURN_STALL_MS=30s) fires on opponent inaction but not on a Realtime delivery gap where the opponent's action was committed but the INSERT notification was lost.
impact: If seq N is lost in Realtime delivery, all subsequent actions buffer silently. The engine stays in PLAYER_TURN (waiting for N), the turn-stall watchdog fires after 30s (a user-visible banner), but there is no log indicating that buffered actions are pending while a gap exists — distinguishing 'opponent idle' from 'Realtime delivery gap' is impossible from logs.
recommendation: Log a warning if pendingActions.size exceeds 3 (more than a small burst) or if flushPendingActions is called but nextExpectedSeq is not in the map while the map is non-empty. This catches gap-stall states: console.warn('pending action gap', { expected: this.nextExpectedSeq, buffered: [...this.pendingActions.keys()] }).
acceptance_criteria: A Realtime delivery gap that stalls the engine emits a diagnosable log line
Log includes the expected seq and what is buffered
